claim: IL-10 production by monocytes encourages CD4 + T cell response.

result.claim: IL-10 production by monocytes encourages CD4 + T cell response.
result.reasoning:
The claim that "IL-10 production by monocytes encourages CD4 + T cell response" is strongly refuted by the available evidence. Paper 10648422, titled "Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection," explicitly states that "Triggering of PD-1 expressed on monocytes by PD-L1 expressed on various cell types induced IL-10 production and led to reversible CD4+ T cell dysfunction." This indicates an inhibitory, rather than encouraging, effect. Furthermore, proposition prop_2311 from the same paper states, "Triggering of PD-1 expressed on monocytes by PD-L1 expressed on various cell types induced IL-10 production." Proposition prop_2312 also from paper 10648422 states, "Triggering of PD-1 expressed on monocytes by PD-L1 expressed on various cell types led to reversible CD4+ T cell dysfunction."

Additionally, paper 10040642, "Inhibitory and Stimulatory Effects of IL-10 on Human CD8+ T Cells," provides further refuting evidence. Proposition prop_2238 from this paper clearly states, "IL-10 inhibits CD4+ T cell proliferation in vitro." The abstract of this paper also mentions that "IL-10 is a well-documented immunosuppressant that inhibits macrophage-dependent Ag presentation and CD4+ T cell proliferation in vitro."

evidence.text: PD-1 expression on monocytes correlates with high plasma concentrations of IL-10 in HIV-infected subjects.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: IL-10 promotes the growth of activated CD8+ T cells in combination with low doses of IL-2.
evidence.source: Inhibitory and Stimulatory Effects of IL-10 on Human CD8+ T Cells
